title: AASTMT Educational Leadership
content: The Arab Academy for Science, Technology and Maritime Transport (AASTMT) is one of the leading educational establishments in the Middle East, and it always had a leading role providing the latest technologies with the most up-to-date educational facilities and resources to ensure that our teaching and research is kept at the leading edge.

title: Global Growth of AI
content: The field of Artificial Intelligence (AI) is developing a lot and has spread over every part of the world at a rapid pace. AI, as one of the most important pillars in science, emphasizes on simulating the human cognitive thinking and behavior in order to automating decision making process or solving cognitive-based problems.

title: AI Emergence and Applications
content: The first appearance of Artificial Intelligence was in 1955 and it was evolved and dominated in many fields that enhanced their performances such as: smart cities, autonomous cars, diseases diagnose, etc.

title: AI Economic Growth Outlook
content: Moreover, many countries has invested in AI focusing on its applications which are expected to increase their average annual gross by 2030.

title: AI Workforce Replacement Outlook
content: Furthermore, AI is also expected to replace the humans in many fields of work by 2060.

title: Need for an AI College
content: Therefore, founding an AI college becomes an essential issue in order to cope with the AI world evolution.

title: Educational and Research Services
content: College of Artificial Intelligence is concerned with providing the latest of technologies and distinguished educational, training and research services, in order to support tomorrow’s technology centric environment and contribute to the solutions of the most challenging future problems.

title: Future Impact of AI
content: Artificial Intelligence concepts and technologies will drive the most critical changes in the whole world for the future.

title: College Vision
content: CAI vision seeks to be a leading and distinguished college in providing the highest quality educational programs, research studies, and community services in the Artificial Intelligence at the national, regional, and international levels

title: Social and Economic Mission
content: CAI mission is to contribute to the social and economic development of the Arab countries by preparing young generations capable of deploying high level knowledge of concepts in Artificial Intelligence and Data Analysis to the real world applications. This can be achieved by providing competitive, intellectual, and society driven academic programs, research studies and community services, while strictly commit to the highest levels of quality.

title: Partnerships and Recognition Goal
content: Furthermore, CAI aims to maintain and develop long term of lasting partnerships with Arab institutions and be internationally recognized.

title: Dean's Welcome Message
content: Dear Students. With great honor comes great responsibility to be the Dean of the College of Artificial Intelligence at Arab academy for Science and Technology and Maritime Transport (AASTMT) in the New El-Alamein City.

title: AASTMT Golden Anniversary
content: The AASTMT, established in 1972, is getting ready to celebrate its gold anniversary in 2022, with a series of events that will commemorate its steady growth, achievements of its students, reputable education, advanced research, and outstanding community service.

title: First AI College in MENA
content: The College of Artificial Intelligence, established in September 2019, is the first established college for Artificial Intelligence in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region.

title: Research and Application Pathways
content: The college provides new pathways for talented students and faculty to research, create, and apply AI and machine learning (ML) across all disciplines.

title: AI as a Study Field
content: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is an area of study in computer science that focuses on creating computer systems that perform tasks that require human intelligence. This can include visual perception, decision-making, machine translation, and even speech recognition.

title: AI Application Areas
content: Current application of AI in key areas include: Robotics process automation, which allows us to emulate and integrate the actions of human with digital systems to be able to reduce labor costs as well as prevent human error; Planning and optimization, which helps us determine necessary steps to complete a series of tasks that can save time and money, and improve safety; Computer vision, which allows us perceiving and learning visual tasks from the world through cameras and sensors; Prediction, which allows us to perform event forecasting and pattern analysis; Natural language understanding by using machines, which will help us to process, analyze, understand, and mimic human language, either spoken or written; Modeling our physical economic and social world to support the study, optimization, and testing of operations through simulation without interfering or interrupting ongoing processes.

title: Pride in Joining the College
content: Dear Students. You must be proud to be admitted to the College of Artificial Intelligence at AASTMT, a prestigious college with the best professors, and curriculum in the field of AI. You decided to join a field of a frontier science that changes so fast and yet affects all aspects of our life. You have started your first step towards a promising career in AI-related fields.

title: Dean's Study Advice
content: Here are some advice to you: Study regularly throughout the semester for all your classes; Set high personal and academic standards for yourself, and live up to them; Do not put off until tomorrow what you can do today. Study should come before pleasure; Believe in yourself and listen to that little voice inside you that says, 'I can do this.'; Stay updated on the latest AI research, not just by getting the information but also by sharing it with others, building on it and making your contribution to field. Your efforts and passion will pay off. Wishing you all the best in what lies ahead and good luck and congratulations! Aly Fahmy

title: AI Development Worldwide
content: Artificial Intelligence (A.I) is developing in a fast pace all over the world as one of the most important pillars in science emphasizing on simulating the human cognitive thinking and behavior in order to automating decision making process or solving cognitive-based problems.

title: AI Economic Output Forecast
content: Moreover, many countries has invested in AI focusing on its applications which are expected to increase the global economic output by 2030 to reach 13 trillion US Dollars (according to a September 2018 by McKinsey).

title: Examples of AI Applications
content: The AI applications, based mainly on simulating the human thinking and providing smart solutions such as self-driving cars and aircrafts, robots which do many tasks of working through human thinking simulations programs, cell-phones' applications that we use continuously, simulation programs that assist in medical diagnosis, Video games' applications based on simulating the human thinking - Supporting decision making in all areas by providing different solutions - smart cities that are established and adopted on technology in various aspects.

title: El Alamein Opening Date
content: College of Artificial Intelligence has been opened its door at El Alamein campus in Sept. 2019

title: Accreditation and Membership
content: The College of Artificial Intelligence (CAI) is accredited by the Supreme Council of Universities in Egypt and is a member of the Arab Network for Quality Assurance in Higher Education (ANQAHE).

title: UClan Cooperation
content: The college maintains a cooperation with the University of Central Lancashire (UClan) in the United Kingdom.

title: Educational Quality Commitment
content: CAI is committed to maintaining high standards of education and continuously improving its programs to meet the needs of students and the industry.

title: Internal and External Evaluations
content: CAI regularly undergoes internal and external evaluations to ensure that it meets the quality standards set by accrediting bodies and to enhance the overall educational experience for its students.

title: AI Labs and Learning
content: The College of Artificial Intelligence (CAI) is equipped with state-of-the-art laboratories that provide students with hands-on experience in various AI technologies and applications. These labs include: The College of Artificial Intelligence offers to the staff and students a combination of basic labs as well as state-of-art research labs.

title: Basic AI Labs
content: The basic labs include: Computer Lab, Data Science Lab, and Robotics Lab.

title: Research AI Labs
content: The research labs include: Natural Language Processing Lab, Computer Vision Lab, and Machine Learning Lab.

title: Lab Hardware and Software
content: These labs are equipped with the latest hardware and software tools to support research and learning in the field of Artificial Intelligence.

title: About UClan
content: The University of Central Lancashire (UClan) is a public university in Lancashire, England, and is known for its strong focus on applied learning and research.

title: UClan Quality Certificate
content: The Offering of Quality Assurance Certificate for the College of Artificial Intelligence

title: Double Graduation Certificate
content: Working on the offering of Graduation Double Certificate one from the University of Central Lancashire and the second from the College of Artificial Intelligence

title: Accredited Summer Studies
content: Offering accredited summer studies for the students of the College of Artificial Intelligence

title: Teaching Participation
content: Participation in the courses teaching activities both Face to Face Education and Online Teaching

title: Joint Research Supervision
content: Having Joint Supervision of Master and PhD research (for both sides)

title: Joint Summer Schools
content: Organizing joint summer schools in Al-Alamein City in the field of artificial intelligence

title: Optional UClan Summer Courses
content: Availability of accredited courses at the University of Central Lancashire during the summer semester, in which students of the College of Artificial Intelligence can enroll on an optional basis

title: Joint Workshops and Conferences
content: Organizing joint workshops and conferences in the field of artificial intelligence

title: EU Project Participation Support
content: Support for the participation in European Union projects

title: Undergraduate Admission Requirements
content: Students must meet the following admission requirements: Completion of secondary education certificate (Math section or Science Section) or equivalent certificates. Meeting the minimum score required to join the college which is announced before the beginning of each semester, in light of the minimum score mandated by AASTMT, as well as the annually announced terms, regulations and qualifying courses per certificate as specified by the AASTMT Supreme Council of Education Affairs. Showing good conduct with no dishonoring judicial sentence issued against them. Submission of all required admission documents. Approval of delegating entities in case of student’s delegation from any country or authority. Passing admission exams determined by AASTMT. Pledging to follow AASTMT rules and regulations.

title: Credit Hour Definition
content: Credit Hours: A Credit Hour for every semester week (CH) is equivalent to one hour of theoretical study (50 minutes), not less than two hours of practical or applied work (50 minutes each) and not less than one hour of individual effort, or three hours of practical or applied work.

title: Semester Course Load
content: The CH determines the course load a student is allowed to register for in each semester. Educational Load: The student is allowed to register for a total of semester CHs depending mainly upon his abilities and examination results. The educational load is usually between 12 CHs (low load) and 18 CHs (full load).

title: Low Load Recommendation
content: Students with low grades in the Secondary Certificate or those who face problems in their study are advised to take the low load CHs in order to help them overcome their educational problems.

title: External Transfer Requirements
content: All transfer applications, both external and internal, are submitted to the Admission and Registration Deanery according to the following: Transfer from Other Institutions: AASTMT accepts students transferred from other institutions provided the university is accredited, the student meets AASTMT admission requirements, and was not dismissed for disciplinary reasons. Transferred students should have completed at least one academic semester elsewhere. Courses may be transferred if equivalent and the grade is at least 'C'; these are not calculated in the GPA but count toward graduation hours, up to a 50% limit.

title: Internal Transfer Rules
content: Optional Transfer within AASTMT: Students may transfer departments or colleges within AASTMT provided they meet admission requirements for the new major, have sufficient time remaining to complete the degree, and previous grades in relevant courses shall be counted toward the new graduation requirements.

title: Academic Year Structure
content: The academic year consists of two regular 15-week semesters (Fall starting in September and Spring starting in February) followed by two weeks of exams.

title: Optional Summer Semester
content: An optional 5-week summer semester starts in June/July followed by one week of exams.

title: Official Academic Calendar
content: The official academic calendar is issued annually by the Academy President.

title: Credit Hour System
content: AASTMT utilizes a credit hour system where courses are assigned specific credit values.

title: GPA Scale Calculation
content: The Grade Point Average (GPA) is calculated on a scale of 0.0 to 4.0 by dividing total points achieved by total registered hours. Points for a course are determined by multiplying the student's score by the course's credit hours.

title: Academic Probation Rule
content: Probation occurs if a GPA falls below 2.0

title: Underachievement Rule
content: Underachievement is triggered if a student completes less than 50% of required credits

title: Forced Withdrawal Rule
content: Forced Withdrawal occurs if absence limits are exceeded.

title: Course Prerequisite Requirement
content: Many courses require Pre-requisites to be completed before registration.

title: Academic Advisor Support
content: Each student is assigned an Academic Advisor to assist with course selection and academic planning.

title: New Student Orientation
content: New students participate in an Orientation Program to learn about registration and rights.

title: Academic Integrity Code
content: All students must adhere to strict Academic Integrity codes.

title: Student Affairs Review
content: Behavioral or ethical offenses are reviewed by the Student Affairs Committee.

title: Semester GPA Calculation
content: Cumulative GPA is calculated at the end of each semester by dividing total acquired points by total registered credit hours.

title: Course Passing Requirements
content: To pass a course, a student must earn at least 50/100 total marks and 12/40 in the final exam.

title: Final Exam Absence Penalty
content: If a student fails to attend the final exam without an excuse, they receive a zero.

title: Failed Course Repeat Policy
content: Students must repeat failed courses, with the maximum grade for success being 3.00 (B).

title: Conditioned Pass Repeat Rule
content: If the cumulative GPA is below 2.00, students must repeat courses with conditioned passes (D) to improve their average.

title: Repeated Course Grade Rule
content: For repeated courses, the highest achieved grade is taken, and credit hours are counted only once in the cumulative GPA.

title: Incomplete Grade Deadline
content: 'Incomplete' grades must be resolved by the first week of the following semester, or the student is considered withdrawn.

title: Program Duration and Semesters
content: The program study duration is 4 years divided into 8 academic semesters and provides graduates with a strong foundation and expertise in all state-of-the-art artificial intelligence technologies and skills.

title: First Four Semesters
content: During the first 4 semesters students study introductory courses in programming, mathematics and core fields in artificial intelligence.

title: Tuition Exemption Scholarship
content: Scholarships providing exemption from tuition fees are awarded each semester to the student with the highest cumulative GPA in each major.

title: Scholarship Eligibility Criteria
content: To qualify, a student must maintain a GPA of at least 3.4, belong to a major with at least 15 students, and have completed all previous study hours without interruption or postponement.

title: Scholarship Major and Summer Rules
content: The scholarship does not apply to the summer semester and requires the student to remain in the same major.

title: Scholarship Tie Rules
content: If multiple students have the same GPA, the scholarship is divided equally, except if multiple students achieve a 4.00, in which case each receives a full scholarship.

title: Transfer Student Scholarship Eligibility
content: Transferred students from abroad are eligible only after completing two semesters in the same major.

title: Contact Information
content: Please feel free to contact us if you need any further information: Email: cai@aast.edu, Whatsapp: +20 1141 6060 29

title: Maximum Grade for Repeated Courses
content: When a student repeats a failed course, the maximum grade they can achieve for that course is 3.00 (B). For any repeated course, the highest achieved grade is the one used in the cumulative GPA calculation, and credit hours are only counted once.

title: UClan Double Degree and Supervision
content: The cooperation with the University of Central Lancashire includes the offering of a Graduation Double Certificate (one from UClan and one from CAI). It also provides for Joint Supervision of Master and PhD research for both institutions.

title: Scholarship Eligibility for Transfer Students
content: Transferred students from abroad or other institutions are only eligible for tuition fee exemption scholarships after they have completed at least two full semesters within the same major at CAI.

title: Admission Score Mandates
content: The minimum scores required to join the college are mandated by the AASTMT Supreme Council of Education Affairs. These terms, regulations, and qualifying courses per certificate are announced annually before each semester.

title: Lab Infrastructure Standards
content: The basic and research labs (including Robotics, Data Science, NLP, and Computer Vision) are equipped with the latest hardware and software tools specifically designed to support advanced research and hands-on learning in Artificial Intelligence.
